run.head: 792a24cd5
gaps: e2e suite not re-run this round: the fix touched only the writer loop in websocket.rs plus one new unit regression test; e2e behavior is unchanged from the prior run (2026-07-22-agent-browser-d549648), where the frame-metadata e2e passed in isolation and the 2 failures were environmental.

provenance.author_model: claude-opus-4-8 (gate runner applied the F1 fix)

lenses.name: cancellation-and-timeout-hygiene
lenses.reason: the fix swaps AtomicU32 for a watch channel and adds a select arm; verified no busy-loop on sender drop (breaks on changed()=Err, mirrors reader_task arm), no leak, single client_count decrement on every exit path

lenses.name: new-domain-matrix
lenses.reason: the config-change input class now includes mid-stream tightening and loosening; loosening fixed (F1) and force-red guarded; tightening surfaced the benign boundary finding F2

lenses.name: error-path-forcing
lenses.reason: new guard changed()=Err -> break forced by reasoning (sender lives for reader lifetime); deadline_from fps=0 past-deadline path exercised by the uncapped tests

lenses.name: deliberate-default-check
lenses.reason: no default-behavior change beyond what the prior run already assessed

lenses.name: docs-behavior-parity
lenses.reason: fix makes config take effect immediately, which is what the docs already imply; no doc contradiction introduced

lenses.name: inverse-regression-surface
lenses.reason: data source unchanged this round (watch channel introduced in prior run, already assessed)

lenses.name: substrate-verification
lenses.reason: force-red regression test asserts on actually-received WS frame timing against a real StreamServer + tokio-tungstenite client

deterministic.reason: no em dash / no ' -- ' prose punctuation in added lines
same skill-data/core exemption as prior run (core skill never documented the WS protocol)
new private helper, all call sites in websocket.rs (in diff)
965 passed, 0 failed, 84 ignored (up 1 from prior run: the new regression test)
test_loosening_cap_midstream_takes_effect_immediately fails at 851ms against the pre-fix code, passes with the fix

findings.claim: Tightening a client's own cap mid-stream (e.g. 0 -> 1 fps) while a frame is pending and the old deadline is already elapsed can let one frame through immediately before the tighter cap applies, because tokio::select! is unbiased and may pick the throttle arm over the config arm on simultaneous readiness.
findings.evidence: Confirmed by code inspection and Codex: on the tie the throttle arm can win. Self-correcting: even when it wins, its final `next_allowed = deadline_from(last_sent, *max_fps_rx.borrow())` reads the already-updated watch value (new fps), so only the single boundary frame is early; the next frame respects the new cap. No frame lost, no stall. Benign direction (client asked for fewer frames, got one extra). Not reproduced deterministically because it is an unbiased-select tie.
findings.layer: writer select! tie-break between the config arm and the throttle arm (websocket.rs:309/323)

exemptions.evidence: unchanged from prior run: config/maxFps is a WS protocol message, not a CLI/MCP surface; core skill never documented the WS protocol.

issue_candidates.title: One frame can exceed a just-tightened maxFps at the transition boundary
issue_candidates.evidence: Unbiased select! tie between throttle and config arms; self-correcting after one frame. Strict enforcement would need a biased select (risks starving other arms) or a re-check in the throttle arm.
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: Benign rate-limiter boundary slop in the harmless direction; does not affect the common set-once-at-connect usage; fix cost/risk exceeds the one-frame benefit. Left as-is by decision.
